title: install latest version of skopeo in ubuntu 22.04
body:
os: ubuntu 22.04
```
apt update
apt install -y skopeo

root@ubuntu:~# skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.4.1
```

now skopeo latest version is 1.8.0 , but the latest ubuntu version 22.04 use 1.4.1 ,There are huge differences between them in four versions， and there no way to install latest 1.8.0 version in skopeo, but why?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

It’s generally up to the individual distributions (and their support/stability policies) to update repository packages. Failing that, building from source might be an option.

@willzhang hello, this project doesn't maintain the Ubuntu 22.04 LTS package and also from what I gather, 22.04 itself will most likely not update packages. In addition to building from source as @mtrmac mentioned, you have some other options:

- use the skopeo container image
- use packages from the debian experimental repo which *may* have newer packages
- UNRELIABLE METHOD: I have some work-in-progress to build skopeo for the latest ubuntu and debian at https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable, but there's zero guarantee that they will ever be fully supported, so please consider one of the other three options.

Hope that helps.

Closing...

thanks ,i find way install latest version of skopeo
```
echo 'deb http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/home:/alvistack/xUbuntu_22.04/ /' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/home:alvistack.list
curl -fsSL https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/home:alvistack/xUbuntu_22.04/Release.key | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/home_alvistack.gpg > /dev/null
sudo apt update
sudo apt install skopeo
```
